name: p2p-networking
description: Peer-to-peer networking patterns using commonware for building decentralized Guts network
capabilities: Implement authenticated peer connections using Ed25519 keypairs
Design message protocols for repository sync and announcements
Build gossip protocols for decentralized message propagation
Configure network topology with bootstrap nodes and peer discovery
Implement peer state management and connection lifecycle
Apply security patterns including encryption and rate limiting
author: AbdelStark
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing instructional content and Rust code templates for P2P networking patterns. Contains no executable code, no file system access, no network operations. Only architectural guidance for implementing decentralized networking using commonware primitives.
valueStatement: Implementing peer-to-peer networking for decentralized platforms requires understanding encrypted communication, gossip protocols, and distributed state management. This skill provides ready-to-use patterns using commonware for authenticated, encrypted P2P connections.
userTitle: Build P2P networking for decentralized apps
limitations: Does not provide pre-built binaries or deployment scripts
Does not include consensus algorithm implementation
Does not integrate with specific cloud providers
Does not handle storage layer implementation

useCases.title: Build decentralized networks
useCases.description: Implement P2P networking layer for new decentralized applications using commonware primitives

useCases.title: Design gossip protocols
useCases.description: Create efficient message propagation systems for distributed state synchronization across nodes

useCases.title: Define message schemas
useCases.description: Design communication protocols for peer discovery, repository sync, and network announcements

promptTemplates.title: Basic peer connection
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I establish authenticated peer connections using Ed25519 in a P2P network? Show me the handshake process.

promptTemplates.title: Repository sync
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement repository synchronization between peers. How do I find peers with a repository and fetch missing objects?

promptTemplates.title: Gossip protocol
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a gossip protocol for broadcasting messages. How do I prevent message duplication and select peers for propagation?

promptTemplates.title: Network configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure a P2P network with bootstrap nodes, connection timeouts, and keep-alive intervals. What security considerations should I apply?

bestPractices: Always authenticate peers using cryptographic keypairs before establishing connections
Implement message validation and rate limiting to prevent denial-of-service attacks
Use gossip protocols with deduplication to efficiently propagate updates without flooding the network
antiPatterns: Storing peer state without connection health checks or timeout mechanisms
Allowing unlimited connections without maximum connection limits
Processing messages without validating their structure and source

faq.question: Which platforms support this skill?
faq.answer: Compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Works with any AI assistant that can understand Rust patterns.

faq.question: What transport protocols are supported?
faq.answer: Uses QUIC and TCP transports with Noise XX protocol for encrypted handshakes and Ed25519 authentication.

faq.question: How does peer discovery work?
faq.answer: Uses DHT for distributed peer discovery plus bootstrap nodes for initial network entry and peer announcements.

faq.question: Is my data safe with P2P networking?
faq.answer: All traffic is encrypted with Noise protocol. Peers are authenticated via Ed25519 signatures. Rate limiting and peer scoring prevent abuse.

faq.question: Why are messages not reaching all peers?
faq.answer: Check that bootstrap nodes are accessible and peer scoring is not disconnecting nodes. Ensure message validation passes on receiving peers.

faq.question: How is this different from libp2p?
faq.answer: Commonware provides lighter-weight primitives optimized for performance. Uses QUIC by default and integrates with Simplex BFT consensus.
